div.p: Scenario: Concurrent writes with identical Lamport timestamps. Resolution: Deterministic tiebreaker via Node ID comparison.

ol.li: Deterministic Execution: Every node running the same ID tiebreaker will always pick 'Bob', ensuring global convergence without a coordinator.
Storage Trade-off: 'Alice' write is silently dropped. This is the classic "Last Write Wins" data loss trade-off for high availability.
Node IDs: Must be globally unique (UUID or static machine ID) and assigned at boot to prevent non-deterministic outcomes.
